summary: I wrote the business requirements artifact to `.pipeliner/phases/01-define/main/requirements-writer/output/requirements.md`. Since `input.json` carried no discovery notes, I grounded the requirements in the ask plus the domain vocabulary from `docs/concepts.md` and `docs/data-model.md` (pipeline statuses and phases, worker fleet states, stuck-work detection, activity events like approvals and rework). The result is 35 atomic, numbered requirements (R1–R35) in plain "When X happens, Y should happen" language, organized into access/landing, overall layout and at-a-glance summary, active-pipelines overview, recent activity, worker fleet health, freshness/live updates, and responsiveness/accessibility — deliberately keeping the dashboard a read-and-navigate surface, covering empty and unhealthy states, and requiring that status never be conveyed by color alone.
